origin.question: Problem 19.15. For a Friedmann cosmology derive the relations$$\frac {8 \pi G \rho}{3} = \left(\frac {\mathrm {k}}{\mathrm {R}^{2}} + \mathrm {H}^{2}\right) \tag {1}$$$$- 8 \pi G p = \frac {k}{R^{2}} + H^{2} (1 - 2 q). \tag {2}$$If the cosmology is matter-dominated $(\rho >> p)$ show that$$\frac {\mathrm {k}}{\mathrm {R}^{2}} = (2 \mathrm {q} - 1) \mathrm {H}^{2} \tag {3}$$$$\frac {8 \pi G \rho}{3} = 2 q H^{2}. \tag {4}$$If it is radiation dominated $(\mathfrak{p} \approx 1/3 \rho)$ show that$$\frac {k}{R^{2}} = (q - 1) H^{2} \tag {5}$$$$\frac {8 \pi G \rho}{3} = q H^{2}. \tag {6}$$
origin.solution: Solution 19.15. Equation (1) follows immediately from the definition $\mathbf{H} \equiv \dot{\mathbf{R}} / \mathbf{R}$ and the first-order Friedmann equation (Problem 19.14)$$\dot {\mathbf {R}}^{2} + \mathbf {k} = \frac {8 \pi \mathbf {G}}{3} \rho \mathbf {R}^{2}.$$If we now take $\mathrm{d} / \mathrm{d}\mathbf{R}$ on this equation, use the identity $\frac{1}{2}\mathrm{d}[(\dot{\mathbf{R}})^2] / \mathrm{d}\mathbf{R} = \ddot{\mathbf{R}}$ , the other first order equation $\mathrm{d}(\rho \mathbf{R}^3) / \mathrm{d}\mathbf{R} = -3\mathbf{p}\mathbf{R}^2$ and the definition $\mathbf{q} \equiv -\ddot{\mathbf{R}}\mathbf{R} / \dot{\mathbf{R}}^{2}$ , we get Equation (2). If $\rho >> \mathfrak{p}$ then the left side of Equation (2) is negligible compared with the right side, and Equation (3) results. Equation (4) is immediate upon substituting Equation (3) into Equation (1). If $\mathfrak{p} = 1 / 3\rho$ , then eliminating $\rho$ from Equations (1) and (2) gives Equation (5), while eliminating the $\mathbf{k} / \mathbf{R}^2$ term gives Equation (6).

ricci_tensor.R_11: \frac{- 2 k - a{\left(t \right)} \frac{d^{2}}{d t^{2}} a{\left(t \right)} - 2 \left(\frac{d}{d t} a{\left(t \right)}\right)^{2}}{k r^{2} - 1}
ricci_tensor.R_22: r^{2} \left(2 k + a{\left(t \right)} \frac{d^{2}}{d t^{2}} a{\left(t \right)} + 2 \left(\frac{d}{d t} a{\left(t \right)}\right)^{2}\right)
ricci_tensor.R_33: r^{2} \left(2 k + a{\left(t \right)} \frac{d^{2}}{d t^{2}} a{\left(t \right)} + 2 \left(\frac{d}{d t} a{\left(t \right)}\right)^{2}\right) \sin^{2}{\left(\theta \right)}

cached_fact_sheet.kretschmann: \frac{2 \left(2 r^{4} \left(- k r^{2} + 1\right) a^{4}{\left(t \right)} \left(\frac{d^{2}}{d t^{2}} a{\left(t \right)}\right)^{2} + r^{2} \left(k + \left(\frac{d}{d t} a{\left(t \right)}\right)^{2}\right)^{2} \left(k r^{2} - 1\right) + r^{2} \left(k r^{2} - 1\right) \left(2 r^{2} \left(k + \left(\frac{d}{d t} a{\left(t \right)}\right)^{2}\right)^{2} \left(- k r^{2} + 1\right) + \left(k + \left(\frac{d}{d t} a{\left(t \right)}\right)^{2}\right)^{2} + \left(k r^{2} - 1\right) \left(\frac{d^{2}}{d t^{2}} a{\left(t \right)}\right)^{2}\right) + r^{2} a^{4}{\left(t \right)} \left(\frac{d^{2}}{d t^{2}} a{\left(t \right)}\right)^{2} - 2 \left(k + \left(\frac{d}{d t} a{\left(t \right)}\right)^{2}\right)^{2} + 2 \left(- k r^{2} + 1\right) \left(\frac{d^{2}}{d t^{2}} a{\left(t \right)}\right)^{2}\right)}{r^{2} \left(k r^{2} - 1\right) a^{4}{\left(t \right)}}
